apps.overview:
## Overview

CloudCasa is a Kubernetes backup, recovery, DR, and migration solution for Nutanix Kubernetes Platform and all other
major Kubernetes distributions and managed services.

Built for NKP users seeking a powerful, easy-to-use, and scalable solution, CloudCasa combines Nutanix’s high-performance
infrastructure with intuitive, Kubernetes-native backup, migration, and DR capabilities.
Its enterprise-ready design includes full multi-tenancy and RBAC-driven self-service to support platform teams, business units, and service providers.

## Key Features

o Kubernetes-native backups with file-level and resource-level recovery.

o Manage cross-cluster, cross-tenant, cross-region and cross-cloud backups and recoveries from a single dashboard.

o Seamless application migration between NKP clusters, whether on-prem or in the cloud.

o Hybrid restore options for rapid failover to secondary Nutanix sites or public cloud clusters.

o Back up to Nutanix Objects, S3 and S3-cpmpatible object storage, Azure Blob storage, or NFS/CIFS file storage.

o Support for immutable backups to protect against ransomware and satisfy compliance requirements.

o Multi-tenant support with granular RBAC to safely delegate backup and restore operations.

o Available as SaaS or a self-hosted deployment.

## More Information

**[Sign up](https://cloudcasa.io/) to try CloudCasa for free**

**See the [CloudCasa User Guide]( https://docs.cloudcasa.io/help/)**

**See our [CloudCasa for NKP](https://cloudcasa.io/partners/nutanix-kubernetes-platform-backup-migration-and-dr/) page**

apps.name: kasm
apps.description: Stream your workspace directly to your web browser on any device and from any location.
apps.overview:
## Overview

Kasm Workspaces provides secure, containerized, and browser-accessible workspaces for a variety of applications. It delivers isolated and secure environments that can be accessed via web browsers, eliminating the need for local installations and enhancing security by keeping sensitive data within your infrastructure. Kasm is ideal for providing VDI, remote browser access, and secure access to development environments.

The Kasm Helm chart simplifies the deployment of a Kasm Workspaces instance on Kubernetes, enabling rapid deployment and management of your virtual workspace infrastructure.

## Highlights

- **Browser-Based Access:** Access workspaces from any modern web browser, regardless of the user's operating system.
- **Containerized Environments:** Provides isolated and secure workspaces using Docker containers.
- **Centralized Management:** Manage and scale your workspace infrastructure from a single control plane.
- **Enhanced Security:** Keep sensitive data within your infrastructure and leverage Kasm's security features.
- **Simplified Deployment:** Deploy Kasm Workspaces on Kubernetes with a single Helm chart.

## Getting Started

Set the required values before deploying. **`publicAddr` must be updated to your actual FQDN before enabling:**
```yaml
publicAddr: "<kasm.example.com>"
certificate:
  secretName: "<kasm-tls-secret>"
  certManager:
    enabled: true
    addWildCard: true
    issuerName: "<issuerName>"
    issuerKind: "<issuerKind>"
    issuerGroup: "cert-manager.io"
proxyService:
  type: ClusterIP
ingress:
  enabled: true
```
NKP comes with CertManager but the values for `issuerName`, `issuerKind` needs to be populated.

For more configuration options, checkout [Kasm Helm Github](https://github.com/kasmtech/kasm-helm/blob/release/1.18.1/charts/kasm/values.yaml).

## Post-Deployment

Once deployed, ensure your DNS is pointed to the cluster ingress and access Kasm at `https://<publicAddr>`.

To retrieve credentials, run:
```bash
NS=$(kubectl get helmrelease -A | grep kasm | awk '{print $1}') && \
RELEASE=$(kubectl get helmrelease -A | grep kasm | awk '{print $1"-"$2}') && \
kubectl get secret -n $NS -l "owner=helm,name=$RELEASE,status=deployed" -o jsonpath='{.items[0].data.release}' | base64 -d | base64 -d | gunzip | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['info']['notes'])"
```

A Kasm Agent is required to start a Kasm session. The agent must be installed separately on a dedicated VM. Refer to the [Kasm Agent Installation Guide](https://docs.kasm.com/docs/1.18.1/install/multi_server_install#install-agent-server-roles) for setup instructions.

## Resources

- [Kasm Workspaces Documentation](https://docs.kasm.com/)
- [Kasm Helm Github](https://github.com/kasmtech/kasm-helm/tree/release/1.18.1)

apps.name: kubex-automation-stack
apps.description: Kubex data collection and automation stack for Kubernetes environments.
apps.overview:
# Kubex Automation Stack

The **Kubex Automation Stack** deploys the core enterprise components required for continuous Kubex data collection, machine-learning analysis, and autonomous resource optimization within Kubernetes environments.

Powered by **Densify’s** analytics engine, the stack bridges the gap between infrastructure efficiency and application performance by automating cloud cost optimization (FinOps) and workload right-sizing.

---

## 🚀 Key Capabilities

* **Autonomous AI Resource Optimization:** Continuously analyzes container utilization patterns to safely automate CPU and memory right-sizing, eliminating slack without risking application stability.
* **Advanced GPU Optimization:** Dynamically manages dense AI/ML workloads, enabling efficient GPU sharing, scheduling, and scaling.
* **Intelligent Data Collection:** Deploys lightweight collectors that integrate seamlessly with cluster telemetry (e.g., Prometheus) to capture granular utilization metrics with zero performance impact.
* **Platform-Wide Visibility:** Provides SRE, Platform Engineering, and FinOps teams with actionable recommendations and audit trails for cluster resource footprints.

---

## 🛠 Target Workloads & Substrates

The automation stack operates cluster-wide to manage multiple substrate types:
* **Traditional Microservices:** Standard stateless/stateful application pods.
* **AI/ML Workloads:** High-density, resource-intensive large language models (LLMs) and training pipelines requiring strict GPU governance.

apps.name: traefik-hub
apps.description: Cloud-Native API Intelligence & Management Platform with GitOps-Native Governance
apps.overview:
## Description

Traefik delivers unified API intelligence and governance across both Nutanix AHV virtual machines and Nutanix Kubernetes Platform (NKP) containers. The Nutanix-Traefik partnership extends Nutanix infrastructure excellence into comprehensive application intelligence, providing identity-aware routing, comprehensive security, policy-driven governance, end-to-end observability, and AI runtime policy management.

Traefik in NKP provides a progressive pathway for organizations to evolve their API strategy—from fundamental application routing to sophisticated API Gateway functionality, AI Gateway services, and comprehensive API lifecycle management. Deploy with one click from the NKP catalog to gain automatic service discovery, GitOps-native management, and policy-as-code governance across both VM and container workloads.

**[Request a trial license](https://info.traefik.io/try-traefik-hub-platform)** to get started.

## Critical Capabilities

**Automatic Service Discovery**
Traefik integrates directly with Prism Central for automatic VM discovery and category-based grouping. For containers, Traefik provides out-of-the-box service discovery that automatically detects new services as they're deployed, with zero-configuration reducing operational overhead.

**GitOps-Native Management**
Full deployment and management automation is achieved through Ansible and Terraform integrations, enabling repeatable deployments across environments with GitOps workflows for version control and rollback capabilities.

**Policy-as-Code Governance**
The platform leverages OIDC, mTLS, and JWT processing to enforce authentication and authorization policies that span VM and container boundaries. Built-in WAF protection blocks common attacks like SQL injection and cross-site scripting using Traefik's Coraza engine.

**Comprehensive Observability**
OpenTelemetry integration provides distributed tracing, metrics, and audit-grade logging. Traces span VM and container boundaries across Nutanix infrastructure, providing end-to-end visibility into request flows.

**Advanced Traffic Management**
The platform provides circuit-breaking, retry logic, and canary deployment capabilities. Distributed rate limiting operates across all Traefik instances. Traffic shaping includes circuit breaking, retries, and timeouts.

**Multi-Protocol Support**
Native handling of HTTP/HTTPS, gRPC, WebSocket, TCP, and UDP traffic.

## Challenges Addressed

**Duplicate Tools and Fragmented Operations**
Running separate paths for VMs and Kubernetes multiplies tools, fragments policy, and slows audits. Gateways, WAFs, and load balancers are paid for twice and managed twice, with routing rules and policies rebuilt in two places.

**Security Gaps and Inconsistent Policies**
Auth, rate limits, and WAF rules drift across VM and Kubernetes paths. Logs live in different systems, so audits take longer and miss context, with no single policy source of truth.

**Manual API Management**
Service discovery becomes manual and cumbersome, with team resources limited for managing infrastructure complexity. Security requirements demand centralized authentication and authorization across multiple teams.

**AI and API Delivery Complexity**
Model safeguards and API controls are inconsistent across environments. Extra network hops and sidecars add latency and break SLOs.

**Migration and Modernization Challenges**
Moving from ingress to full gateway features triggers rework. Edge and remote sites evolve on their own and create surprises, with VM admins and platform engineers using different playbooks.

**Hybrid Infrastructure Management**
The hybrid reality persists: virtual machines and containers will coexist for years, and organizations need unified application delivery that spans compute substrates and eliminates tool sprawl.

## Why Traefik API Management on Nutanix?

**Unified Fabric for VMs and Containers**
Traefik connects to Prism Central, discovers services across AHV and NKP, and applies one set of policies for routing, security, and governance everywhere, making VMs and Kubernetes feel like one platform.

**Nutanix Ready Validated Integration**
This Nutanix Ready validated integration eliminates the complexity of managing separate tools for different compute substrates while providing enterprise-grade Layer 7 services. Customers manage application delivery through familiar Nutanix constructs including Prism Central, Flow networking, and category-based policies.

**Infrastructure-as-Code Approach**
Organizations can define Traefik configurations, routing policies, and security rules as code using the same tools Nutanix customers use for infrastructure provisioning—Ansible and Terraform.

**Zero-Dependency Architecture**
The platform supports both connected and air-gapped deployments through signed policy bundles. Traefik operates entirely within Nutanix infrastructure with all routing logic, security enforcement, and policy decisions executing locally without requiring connectivity to external control planes.

**Progressive Capability Evolution**
Traefik provides a progressive pathway allowing enterprises to advance at their own pace, adopting capabilities sequentially as needs mature, without disruptive migrations or platform changes. Organizations start with Application Proxy, advance to API Gateway, and evolve to full API Management.

**AI Workload Governance**
For AI workloads running on Nutanix infrastructure, the integration provides NVIDIA Safety NIMs integration, enabling enterprise-controlled AI deployment without external cloud dependencies. The Traefik AI Gateway capability supports model routing governance.

## Customer Benefits

**Simplified Operations**
Run one gateway for AHV VMs and NKP clusters with shared rules and routes, keeping the same traffic shaping, retries, splits, and transformations across all apps. VM admins and platform teams gain one workflow and one dashboard.

**Reduced Costs and Tool Consolidation**
Consolidate point tools and reduce total cost by eliminating duplicate gateways, WAFs, and load balancers. Every upgrade, license, and support ticket costs less.

**Accelerated Deployment**
Zero-configuration service discovery reduces operational overhead with real-time traffic routing adjustments. Simplified configuration through Kubernetes CRDs and annotations provides a centralized entry point.

**Enhanced Security and Compliance**
Centralize auth and authorization so policies match in VM and Kubernetes paths. Enforce WAF and safeguards with unified logs for faster, cleaner audits. Works in sovereign and air-gapped environments under complete customer control.

**Faster Problem Resolution**
OpenTelemetry integration provides distributed tracing and audit-grade logging. Traces span VM and container boundaries, providing end-to-end visibility regardless of where application components execute.

**Hybrid Cloud Flexibility**
Standardize one policy and observability plane across datacenter, edge, and cloud. Route AI alongside APIs with the same controls and protections. The platform works across edge, Nutanix Cloud Clusters, private cloud, and air-gapped environments with complete operational independence.

**Future-Ready Architecture**
Organizations can evolve their API strategy at their own pace while maintaining a consistent platform and operational model, from microservices to sophisticated API ecosystems with AI components.

---

**Get Started**: Deploy Traefik API Management from the NKP catalog in minutes to unlock comprehensive API intelligence for applications across Nutanix infrastructure. **[Request a trial license](https://info.traefik.io/try-traefik-hub-platform)** to get started.

**[Documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik-hub/introduction/overview)** is available to help you get started with Traefik Hub on NKP.

Override the default license token in the configuration values:
```yaml
hub:
  token: "XXX" # Request a trial license to get started on https://info.traefik.io/try-traefik-hub-platform
```

apps.name: ndk
apps.description: Nutanix Data Services for Kubernetes
apps.overview:
# Overview
Nutanix Data Services for Kubernetes (NDK) simplifies and unifies the life cycle management of business-critical applications by extending enterprise data services to containerized applications.
## For Information on Features
- [Nutanix Data Services for Kubernetes](https://portal.nutanix.com/page/documents/details?targetId=Nutanix-Data-Services-for-Kubernetes-v2_1:Nutanix-Data-Services-for-Kubernetes-v2_1)

## Prerequisites and Configuration Guide
### This application is supported only on NKP clusters backed by Nutanix Infrastructure.
### Nutanix Data Services for Kubernetes 2.1 requires the following applications to be installed on the cluster:
- cert-manager v1.13.0 or higher
- csi v3.3.8 or higher

### While enabling Nutanix Data Services for Kubernetes from UI, image pull credentials need to be provided. If the secret object already exists in ntnx-system namespace, it's reference can be provided:
```
imageCredentials:
  # Name of the image pull secret
  imagePullSecretName: <name of the image pull secret>
```
### Or credentials can be provided with below configuration:
```
imageCredentials:
  # Image registry credentials
  credentials:
    registry: https://index.docker.io/v1/
    username:
    password:
    email:
```
### TLS will be disabled by default. To enable TLS, please follow the section below.
### For using self-signed certificates (to be issued by existing cert-manager)
```
tls:
  server:
    enable: true
    clusterName: <Name of NKP cluster>
```
### For using a custom issuer (a pre-existing issuer in ntnx-system namespace)
```
tls:
  server:
    enable: true
    mode: "ISSUER"
    issuer:
      issuerName: <issuer-name>
    clusterName: <Name of NKP cluster>
```
### For using a custom Secret (a pre-existing secret in ntnx-system namespace)
```
tls:
  server:
    enable: true
    mode: "SECRET"
    secretName: <Name of pre-existing secret>
    clusterName: <Name of NKP cluster>
```
### For enabling MTLS between NDK intercom service running on source and replication kubernetes clusters, make sure that same certificate authority (CA) signs and issues certificates to NDK intercom service on primary and replication clusters.
To configure MTLS, the following change in configuration should be made:
```
tls:
  server:
    enableMTLS: true
    mode: "SECRET"
    secretName: <Name of pre-existing secret>
    clusterName: <Name of NKP cluster>
```

apps.name: nvidia-gpu-operator
apps.description: The NVIDIA GPU Operator manages NVIDIA GPU resources in a Kubernetes cluster and automates tasks related to bootstrapping GPU nodes.
apps.overview:
# Overview
The NVIDIA GPU Operator uses the operator framework within Kubernetes to automate the management of all NVIDIA software components needed to provision GPU. These components include the NVIDIA drivers (to enable CUDA), Kubernetes device plugin for GPUs, the NVIDIA Container Toolkit, automatic node labelling using GFD, DCGM based monitoring and others.

## More Information
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator Documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/gpu-operator/overview.html)
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator - GitHub](https://github.com/NVIDIA/gpu-operator)

apps.name: nvidia-network-operator
apps.description: The NVIDIA Network Operator simplifies the provisioning and management of NVIDIA networking resources in a Kubernetes cluster.
apps.overview:
# Overview
The operator automatically installs the required host networking software - bringing together all the needed components to provide high-speed network connectivity. These components include the NVIDIA networking driver, Kubernetes device plugin, CNI plugins, IP address management (IPAM) plugin and others.

## More Information
  - [NVIDIA Network Operator Documentation](https://docs.nvidia.com/networking/display/kubernetes2610/index.html)
  - [NVIDIA Network Operator - GitHub](https://github.com/Mellanox/network-operator/tree/master)
